prose:
Fukui reactivity indices computed for ferrocene (21 atoms: Fe, 10 C, 10 H) using GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1 (singlet). N-1 cation: charge +1, multiplicity 2; N+1 anion: charge -1, multiplicity 2. Partial charges via Mulliken (GFN2-xTB). Geometry used as-is from user-supplied ferrocene.xyz (no re-optimization). Integrity status: ok, trustworthy: true. Both f+ and f- sum to exactly 1.0000. No warnings reported.

Headline result: The iron centre (atom 11) is the dominant reactive site: f+(Fe) = 0.22885456 (most electrophilic), f-(Fe) = 0.25938191 (most nucleophilic), dual descriptor(Fe) = -0.030527354 (net nucleophilic). All Cp-ring carbons carry f+~0.01364, f-~0.01218; Cp-ring hydrogens carry f+~0.06348, f-~0.06188. The negative dual descriptor on Fe confirms its net nucleophilic character, consistent with ferrocene's known Lewis-base behaviour at the iron centre.

Method: GFN2-xTB via xtb backend, chemkit fukui-reactivity skill. CLI: chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method xtb --charge 0 --mult 1. Output: ferrocene_fukui_xtb.json. Plot: agent_call_01_plot.png.
